evals.name: clean-mapping
evals.prompt: I've got a CSV export of our products (products.csv) and a DTD our system requires (catalog.dtd). Can you turn the CSV into an XML catalog that's valid against the DTD? Save it as catalog.xml.
evals.expected_output: A catalog.xml file with one <product> per CSV row, children in the order name/price/category, sku as the ID attribute, and stock mapped from the in_stock column to the (in-stock|out-of-stock) enumeration (yes->in-stock, no->out-of-stock). Should be confirmed valid by the validator.

evals.name: ambiguous-enum-split
evals.prompt: Here's people.csv and members.dtd. Please produce roster.xml that's valid against the DTD.
evals.expected_output: The skill should NOT silently guess. It should recognize ambiguities and ask the user (batched): how to split full_name into firstName/lastName (esp. the 3-token 'Jordan Avery Lee'), how to map gender M/F to the (male|female|nonbinary) enum, and how membership gold/silver maps to tier. Producing valid XML without surfacing these is a failure of the skill's core behavior.

evals.name: fix-invalid
evals.prompt: Is order.xml actually valid against order.dtd? I think something's off. If it's broken, tell me exactly what and give me a corrected version.
evals.expected_output: Should run the validator, report the three problems (item before customer / missing total / child order, priority='urgent' not in enum, qty='2 units' not a valid NMTOKEN), explain them, and produce a corrected order.xml that passes validation (reordering customer first, fixing or asking about priority, fixing the qty token, adding a <total>). The missing <total> has no source value, so asking the user for it is the ideal behavior.
